stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking of a major LLM from Meta.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest Llama iteration.

stories.title: Microsoft’s next-gen quantum chip cuts timeline to useful quantum computing
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is advancing its quantum computing efforts with the announcement of Majorana 2.
Majorana 2 is the next-generation topological quantum chip, succeeding the initial Majorana 1 processor.
The development aims to accelerate the timeline toward achieving useful quantum computing capabilities.

stories.title: Microsoft’s first advanced reasoning AI is here
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Microsoft unveiled its first advanced, in-house reasoning AI model, MAI-Thinking-1, at Build 2026.
This marks a significant strategic shift for Microsoft, moving away from exclusive reliance on OpenAI's models.
The announcement follows recent renegotiations in the partnership between Microsoft and OpenAI.

stories.title: Microsoft's Project Solara is an Android OS designed for agents instead of apps
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Microsoft is developing Project Solara, a new Android OS.
The OS is fundamentally designed around AI 'agents' rather than traditional mobile 'apps'.
This signals a potential industry shift in how mobile operating systems will function in the age of AI.

stories.title: CoEval: Ranking Language Models for Custom Tasks Without Labeled Data or Trustworthy Benchmarks
stories.author: Yehudit Aperstein
bullet_points: Introduces CoEval, a framework designed to rank Language Models (LMs) for custom tasks.
Addresses the critical problem of evaluating LMs when labeled data is unavailable and standard benchmarks are untrustworthy (due to potential pretraining data leakage).
Uses teacher models to synthesize fresh, attribute-controlled benchmarks directly from a task description, eliminating the need for human labeling.

stories.title: Gender-Dependent Diagnostic Substitution in LLM Medical Triage: Same Symptoms, Unequal Urgency
stories.author: Qi Han Wong
bullet_points: Research investigates gender bias in LLM medical triage, testing if identical neurological symptoms yield different urgency recommendations based on patient gender.
The study compares three major LLM families (Gemini 3.5 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.4-mini) across varied demographic profiles.
Findings suggest LLMs may exhibit gender-dependent diagnostic substitution, raising serious ethical concerns for clinical deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a large language model (LLM).
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the active LLM development ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML technologies, specifically text-generation pipelines.
Associated tags indicate the use of transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, enhancing local AI capabilities on Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is running AI capabilities entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or via the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate new gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed specifically for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in 20B and 120B sizes and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.

stories.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Trump signed an executive order establishing a voluntary framework for AI companies.
The framework requires sharing frontier AI models with the federal government prior to public release.
The stated goals are to promote secure innovation and enhance the cybersecurity of critical infrastructure.

stories.title: Amazon-owned Ring should pay Americans for scanning their faces, lawsuit says
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Ring cameras, owned by Amazon, are reportedly using AI to scan and identify faces of guests and passersby.
A lawsuit alleges that Ring is collecting biometric data from Americans without proper compensation or consent.
The story highlights growing legal and ethical concerns surrounding facial recognition technology in consumer smart home devices.

stories.title: Android phones will soon be able to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Android is receiving a new feature drop that enhances scam detection capabilities.
The update specifically targets the ability of phones to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams.
The release bundles this security enhancement with other updates, including more AirDrop functionality and AI integration.

stories.title: Diagnosing Knowledge Gaps in LLM Tool Use: An Agentic Benchmark for Novel API Acquisition
stories.author: Hongyi Wen
bullet_points: Addresses a critical limitation in LLM tool use: the ability to effectively use novel APIs not present in training data.
Introduces NovelAPIBench, a dynamic benchmark designed to test complex API acquisition skills (signatures, contracts, usage patterns).
Moves beyond static or coarse-grained testing by simulating real-world library evolution and complex coordination tasks for LLMs.

stories.title: Black-box, Adaptive, Efficient, Transferable, Harmful, Applicable... Attacks Are All You Need to Break LLMs
stories.author: Leo Schwinn
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical lack of standardized adversarial robustness evaluation methods for Large Language Models (LLMs), unlike image classifiers.
It highlights that current flawed attack designs can lead to inflated robustness estimates, undermining risk assessment and defense comparisons.
The research focuses on the need for comprehensive, transferable, and effective attack methodologies to accurately test LLM security.

stories.title: Black-box, Adaptive, Efficient, Transferable, Harmful, Applicable... Attacks Are All You Need to Break LLMs
stories.author: Leo Schwinn
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical lack of standardized adversarial robustness evaluation for Large Language Models (LLMs), unlike image classifiers.
It highlights that current flawed attack designs can lead to unreliable robustness estimates, skewing risk assessment and defense comparisons.
The research focuses on the need for a comprehensive, equivalent to AutoAttack, methodology for LLM jailbreak evaluation.

stories.title: CoEval: Ranking Language Models for Custom Tasks Without Labeled Data or Trustworthy Benchmarks
stories.author: Yehudit Aperstein
bullet_points: Introduces CoEval, a framework designed to rank language models for custom tasks without requiring labeled data.
Addresses the critical issue of unreliable public benchmarks, which often reflect model memorization rather than true task fitness.
Uses teacher models to synthesize fresh, attribute-controlled benchmarks solely from a task or domain description.

stories.title: MAI-Code-1-Flash
stories.author: EvanZhouDev
bullet_points: Microsoft is launching seven new MAI models, indicating continued investment in their AI ecosystem.
MAI-Code-1-Flash is a specific new model released, suggesting advancements in code generation or related tasks.
The release provides technical documentation via a Model Card, offering insight into the model's capabilities and usage.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has expanded its compatibility to include the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage advanced models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature supports running powerful, proprietary-like models alongside open-source alternatives.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code locally.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives (e.g., gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b) instead of proprietary models.

stories.title: I held the next-gen handheld
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Intel is facing significant industry challenges, including layoffs and performance issues that have driven users to competitors like AMD.
Despite broader struggles, Intel's new Panther Lake laptop chip, manufactured on the 18A process, has shown excellent performance.
The article highlights the release of a next-generation handheld device utilizing this new Intel technology.

stories.title: Microsoft plans Linux tools and an RTX Spark desktop for Windows developers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft is expanding its support for Linux tools within its ecosystem.
The announcement includes plans for a new RTX Spark desktop specifically aimed at Windows developers.
The news highlights several software updates and initiatives presented at Microsoft Build.

stories.title: Mathematicians warn of AI threats to profession as industry encroaches
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Mathematicians have issued warnings regarding the growing influence of the tech industry on their profession.
The International Mathematical Union (IMU) has formally endorsed these concerns.
The core issue revolves around the encroachment of industry practices and AI on mathematical practice.

stories.title: From Answers to States: Verifiable Process-Level Evaluation of Chemical Reasoning in Large Language Models
stories.author: Li Yuan
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in LLM evaluation for chemistry: current benchmarks only check final answers, ignoring flawed reasoning.
Introduces ChemCoTBench-V2, a new rule-verifiable diagnostic tool to evaluate the process-level reasoning of LLMs in chemistry.
Aims to overcome the scalability and inconsistency issues associated with human or LLM-based step-level evaluation.

stories.title: Safety Measurements for Fine-tuned LLMs Should be Grounded in Capability
stories.author: Svetlana Kiritchenko
bullet_points: Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) risks compromising their inherent safety features.
The paper argues that safety evaluations during fine-tuning must be grounded in specific capability goals.
This approach aims to move beyond arbitrary experimental settings to draw meaningful, comparable conclusions about safety impacts.

stories.title: VidMsg: A Benchmark for Implicit Message Inference in Short Videos
stories.author: Rami Ben-Ari
bullet_points: Introduces VidMsg, a new benchmark specifically designed for evaluating implicit message inference in short online videos.
The benchmark comprises 400 YouTube-derived clips categorized across 9 practical topic areas and 52 fine-grained target messages.
This research addresses the need to understand the underlying purpose or message in short, internet-native video content, moving beyond simple object detection.

stories.title: AnchorMoE: Interpretable Time Series Classification via Anchor-Routed MoE
stories.author: Yiu-ming Cheung
bullet_points: Introduces AnchorMoE, a new model for Multivariate Time Series Classification (MTSC).
Focuses on improving interpretability in MTSC, crucial for high-stakes applications like clinical diagnosis.
Addresses the challenge of sparse and noisy discriminative signals in real-world time series data.

stories.title: TSQAgent: Rating Time Series Data Quality via Dedicated Agentic Reasoning
stories.author: See-Kiong Ng
bullet_points: The paper introduces TSQAgent, an agentic reasoning approach to assess the quality of time series (TS) data.
It addresses the limitation of existing LLM-based TS quality assessment, which relies on manually predefined quality dimensions.
The research explores whether LLMs can autonomously identify relevant quality dimensions and perform deeper reasoning in TS data quality evaluation.

stories.title: AnchorMoE: Interpretable Time Series Classification via Anchor-Routed MoE
stories.author: Yiu-ming Cheung
bullet_points: Introduces AnchorMoE, a new model for Multivariate Time Series Classification (MTSC).
Focuses on improving interpretability in MTSC, crucial for high-stakes applications like clinical diagnosis.
Addresses the challenge of sparse and noisy discriminative signals in real-world time series data.

stories.title: Building Reliable Long-Form Generation via Hallucination Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Yarin Gal
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of LLM hallucination, particularly in long-form text generation.
Introduces a novel inference-time framework called Segment-wise HAllucination Rejection Sampling.
Aims to prevent 'hallucination snowballing,' where initial errors compound in lengthy outputs.

stories.title: Physics-Guided Policy Optimization with Self-Distillation
stories.author: Kai Wei
bullet_points: The paper introduces Physics-Guided Policy Optimization (PGPO) to address instability in Self-Distilled Policy Optimization (SDPO) for LLM post-training.
SDPO's sensitivity lies in uniformly trusting self-teacher corrections, which can be misleading on certain data batches.
The proposed method draws inspiration from viscous-fluid dynamics to better regulate the trust and step size of self-distillation updates.

stories.title: A 3D Isovist World Model -- Revealing a City's Unseen Geometry and Its Emergent Cross-City Signature
stories.author: Tao Yang
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in embodied AI world models, which often predict visual appearance rather than navigable space.
The proposed '3D Isovist World Model' aims to capture the true, traversable geometry of a city environment.
This approach moves beyond flattened representations (like occupancy grids) to reveal the full, unseen 3D structure and movement possibilities of a city.

stories.title: Exploiting Verification-Generation Gap: Test-Time Reinforcement Learning with Confidence-Conditioned Verification
stories.author: See-Kiong Ng
bullet_points: The research addresses the 'Verification-Generation Gap' in large language models using Test-Time Reinforcement Learning.
It focuses on optimizing Pass@k performance in label-free settings, which is crucial for measuring generation coverage and sustained exploration.
The work tackles the non-trivial challenge of applying Pass@k advantages in environments lacking explicit labels.

stories.title: CauTion: Knowing When to Trust LLMs for Ensemble Causal Discovery
stories.author: Chaochao Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of causal discovery from observational data, noting limitations of purely statistical methods.
Proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to inject domain knowledge into statistical inference for causal discovery.
Identifies key weaknesses in current LLM-augmented methods, such as vulnerability to LLM errors and high token costs.

stories.title: Safety Measurements for Fine-tuned LLMs Should be Grounded in Capability
stories.author: Svetlana Kiritchenko
bullet_points: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) risks compromising their inherent safety features.
The paper argues that safety evaluation during fine-tuning must be grounded in specific capability goals.
This approach allows for more meaningful conclusions and consistent comparison of safety mitigation methods.

stories.title: Building Reliable Long-Form Generation via Hallucination Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Yarin Gal
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of hallucination in Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly during long-form text generation.
Introduces a novel inference-time framework, 'Segment-wise HAllucination Rejection Sampling,' to mitigate these errors.
Focuses on preventing 'hallucination snowballing,' where initial errors compound in extended outputs.

stories.title: Bridging Auxiliary Constraints to Resolve Instruction Following in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Xian Wu
bullet_points: Addresses the Constraint Adherence Problem (CAP) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs), where models struggle with complex, multi-part instructions.
Proposes a novel framework that models instructions as a structured knowledge graph of constraints.
Focuses on improving the reliability of instruction following by explicitly managing and balancing competing constraints.

stories.title: Beyond the Literal: Decomposing Pragmatic Intent in Multimodal Meme Understanding
stories.author: Yulan He
bullet_points: Current Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) struggle to grasp the true intent behind memes or sarcastic posts, often describing only the literal image content.
The research proposes reframing meme understanding as a 'literal-pragmatic decomposition' problem.
A new framework called 'Intent Projection' is introduced to effectively separate the literal content from the underlying pragmatic meaning.

stories.title: World Models Meet Language Models: On the Complementarity of Concrete and Abstract Reasoning
stories.author: Jianbing Shen
bullet_points: The research explores the complementary nature of World Models and Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
World Models excel at generating concrete, visual simulations of future outcomes, while MLLMs handle abstract reasoning over goals and rules.
A key challenge identified is ensuring the visual rollouts generated by World Models are not only plausible but also task-correct, given their stochastic nature.

stories.title: CauTion: Knowing When to Trust LLMs for Ensemble Causal Discovery
stories.author: Chaochao Lu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of causal discovery from observational data, noting limitations in purely statistical methods.
It proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to inject domain knowledge into causal inference, a promising direction.
A key challenge identified is the vulnerability of current LLM-augmented methods to LLM errors and high computational costs.

stories.title: Can LLM Rerankers Predict Their Own Ranking Performance?
stories.author: Xueqi Cheng
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for estimating retrieval ranking quality (Query Performance Prediction or QPP) before human relevance judgments are available.
The core contribution is investigating 'reranker-internal QPP,' asking if an LLM reranker can predict the quality of its own output ranking.
The study explores both training-free and training-based methods to achieve this self-assessment capability in LLM rerankers.

stories.title: AI outperforms law professors in Stanford Law study
stories.author: berlianta
bullet_points: An AI model demonstrated performance exceeding that of law professors in a Stanford Law study.
The research suggests advanced AI capabilities are beginning to impact complex, nuanced fields like law.
The study is published by Stanford Law, lending academic credibility to the findings.

stories.title: Introducing LlamaStash: a zero-overhead, terminal-native llama.cpp launcher
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: LlamaStash is a new tool designed to launch and manage local LLMs using llama.cpp.
It is presented as a single Rust binary offering multiple interfaces: TUI, CLI, and daemon.
The tool includes an OpenAI-compatible proxy, enhancing compatibility for local LLM usage.

stories.title: How fast is LlamaStash? Overhead, throughput, and a fair comparison with Ollama and LM Studio
stories.author: deepu105
bullet_points: The article provides a reproducible performance benchmark of LlamaStash.
It compares LlamaStash against established tools like Ollama, LM Studio, and raw llama-server.
Testing was conducted across diverse hardware platforms, including AMD APU, Apple Silicon, and NVIDIA.
